rationale: The query's core transferable method is the analysis of Turing instability in activator-inhibitor systems on large random networks, leading to node differentiation via a mode-indexed dispersion relation and mean-field approximation. The candidate studies phase transitions and component sizes in clustered random graphs, focusing on structural properties (e.g., transitivity, percolation) rather than the dynamical bifurcation mechanism of the query. The object differs (networked control agents vs. abstract random graphs), but the methods do not cohere.
